title: rosa viscaria subsp. agrestis
intro: Rosa viscaria subsp. agrestis, also known as the alpine rose or wood rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, where it grows in rocky and sandy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Rosa viscaria subsp. agrestis are often infused in tea or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
